title: Schefflera delavayi
intro: Schefflera delavayi, also known as the Delavay Schefflera, is a plant species belonging to the family Araliaceae, native to the mountainous regions of southern China and northern Vietnam. Traditionally, Schefflera delavayi is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to its use in treating various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents responsible for its medicinal properties include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and lignans, including schisandrins, which have been shown to exhibit potential neuroprotective and vasodilatory effects. In herbal medicine, Schefflera delavayi is often prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its ability to tonify the spleen and nourish the blood.
taxonomy: Schefflera delavayi belongs to the family Araliaceae. It is a species of flowering plant native to China. Schefflera delavayi is classified under the genus Schefflera, which is a group of tropical and subtropical plants. The plant's classification is further divided into several subtaxa, including varieties and cultivars. Schefflera delavayi is also known to be closely related to other Schefflera species in terms of its botanical characteristics.

It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help in reducing swelling and fighting free radicals in the body. The plant has also been used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
